sections.heading: Complications
sections.content: {"disease_related": [{"complication": "Locoregional recurrence", "management": "Up to half of all MCC cases develop locoregional recurrence [14-20]. Median time to recurrence is about 8 to 9 months, with 90% of recurrences occurring within 24 months [17,18,48,138]. Locoregional recurrence is associated with development of distant metastasis and poorer DSS and OS. Treatment options include clinical trial enrollment (preferred), systemic therapy, RT, or surgery [MCC-6]"}, {"complication": "Distant metastasis", "management": "Nearly one-third develop distant metastases [8-15]. Most likely sites include distant lymph nodes or skin, bone/bone marrow, lung/pleura, liver, followed by pancreas, adrenal glands, brain, kidneys, subcutaneous tissue, or muscle [16,72,101,102,138,277-279]. Treatment includes systemic therapy, RT, surgery in highly selective circumstances, or best supportive care [MCC-5]"}, {"complication": "Second primary malignancies", "management": "Patients with MCC are at higher risk for prior, concurrent, or subsequent second primary malignancies including SCC, BCC, melanoma, CLL, NHLs, lung, breast, and kidney cancers [19,23,35,91,94,97-104]. Skin self-examination education is important [MCC-6]"}], "treatment_related": {"chemotherapy": ["Treatment-related adverse events (TRAEs) occurring in 68%-77% of patients receiving systemic therapy", "Grade 3 or 4 AEs occurring in 5%-21% with checkpoint immunotherapies", "Reported rates of toxic death with chemotherapy between 3% and 10%, with patients who are older being at higher risk [234,277,306]", "Platinum-based chemotherapy (carboplatin/cisplatin with etoposide) carries risks of nephrotoxicity, myelosuppression, and ototoxicity", "CAV regimen (cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin/epirubicin, vincristine) carries risks of cardiotoxicity (doxorubicin) and neurotoxicity (vincristine)"], "immunotherapy": ["Treatment-related adverse events (TRAEs) similar for avelumab, pembrolizumab, and nivolumab with rates of 68%-77%", "Grade 3 or 4 AEs occurring in 5%-21%", "Immune-related AEs seen in <20% of patients receiving avelumab, all grade 1 or 2 [282-287,293,295-297]", "Safety profiles for checkpoint immunotherapies are significantly different from cytotoxic therapies; clinician and patient education is critical for safe administration [MCC-D]", "Ipilimumab + nivolumab: Grade 3 or 4 TRAEs observed in 40% (cohort A) and 32% (cohort B) in the randomized phase 2 trial [291]", "TRAE incidence higher for combination ipilimumab + nivolumab than nivolumab monotherapy (47% vs 28%) [292]", "Checkpoint inhibitor-related toxicities require monitoring per NCCN Guidelines for Management of Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities"], "targeted_therapy": ["Pazopanib: No unusual toxicities reported in a case series of 5 patients [319]; generally well tolerated with disease stabilization in 4 patients", "Somatostatin analogs (octreotide LAR): Generally well tolerated; disease stabilization achieved in 47% of patients in a phase II study [316]"], "surgery": ["Surgical margins should be balanced with morbidity of surgery to achieve primary tissue closure and avoid undue delay to adjuvant RT [MCC-C]", "Potential for wound healing complications, especially when close to critical structures (eye, mouth)", "Reconstruction involving extensive tissue movement should be delayed until negative histologic margins verified and SLNB performed if indicated [MCC-C]", "SLNB itself carries risks including lymphedema (particularly with groin nodal basin), wound infection, and seroma formation", "In-transit disease: Hyperthermic isolated limb infusion/perfusion (HILP) carries procedure-specific risks; complete responses reported in 86% of patients but with rates of locoregional recurrence and distant metastasis [315]"], "radiation": ["Radiation treatments should be given by a practicing radiation oncologist with radiation physics support [MCC-B]", "Application of bolus over the primary tumor should be considered; use of surface dosimeters recommended to verify delivered dose [MCC-B]", "Adjuvant RT delays >7 to 8 weeks associated with worse outcomes [240,241]", "Hypofractionation should be avoided in higher risk patients due to limited data [MCC-B]", "Caution using multiple fractions >5 Gy/fraction when targets involve cartilage or bone [MCC-B]", "Lymphedema risk, particularly for patients with existing lymphedema or at high risk (high BMI, groin nodal basin, current smoker) [MCC-B]", "Skin toxicity (radiation dermatitis) is expected during and after treatment"]}}

cards.name: Locoregional recurrence
cards.detail: Up to half of all MCC cases develop locoregional recurrence [14-20]. Median time to recurrence is about 8 to 9 months, with 90% of recurrences occurring within 24 months [17,18,48,138]. Locoregional recurrence is associated with development of distant metastasis and poorer DSS and OS. Treatment options include clinical trial enrollment (preferred), systemic therapy, RT, or surgery [MCC-6]

cards.name: Distant metastasis
cards.detail: Nearly one-third develop distant metastases [8-15]. Most likely sites include distant lymph nodes or skin, bone/bone marrow, lung/pleura, liver, followed by pancreas, adrenal glands, brain, kidneys, subcutaneous tissue, or muscle [16,72,101,102,138,277-279]. Treatment includes systemic therapy, RT, surgery in highly selective circumstances, or best supportive care [MCC-5]

cards.name: Second primary malignancies
cards.detail: Patients with MCC are at higher risk for prior, concurrent, or subsequent second primary malignancies including SCC, BCC, melanoma, CLL, NHLs, lung, breast, and kidney cancers [19,23,35,91,94,97-104]. Skin self-examination education is important [MCC-6]

cards.name: Treatment-related adverse events
cards.detail: Treatment-related adverse events (TRAEs) occurring in 68%-77% of patients receiving systemic therapy

cards.name: Grade 3 or 4 adverse events
cards.detail: Grade 3 or 4 AEs occurring in 5%-21% with checkpoint immunotherapies

cards.name: Toxic death
cards.detail: Reported rates of toxic death with chemotherapy between 3% and 10%, with patients who are older being at higher risk [234,277,306]

cards.name: Nephrotoxicity
cards.detail: Platinum-based chemotherapy (carboplatin/cisplatin with etoposide) carries risks of nephrotoxicity

cards.name: Myelosuppression
cards.detail: Platinum-based chemotherapy (carboplatin/cisplatin with etoposide) carries risks of myelosuppression

cards.name: Ototoxicity
cards.detail: Platinum-based chemotherapy (carboplatin/cisplatin with etoposide) carries risks of ototoxicity

cards.name: Cardiotoxicity
cards.detail: CAV regimen (cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin/epirubicin, vincristine) carries risks of cardiotoxicity (doxorubicin)

cards.name: Neurotoxicity
cards.detail: CAV regimen (cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin/epirubicin, vincristine) carries risks of neurotoxicity (vincristine)

cards.name: Treatment-related adverse events (immunotherapy)
cards.detail: Treatment-related adverse events (TRAEs) similar for avelumab, pembrolizumab, and nivolumab with rates of 68%-77%

cards.name: Grade 3 or 4 adverse events (immunotherapy)
cards.detail: Grade 3 or 4 AEs occurring in 5%-21%

cards.name: Immune-related adverse events
cards.detail: Immune-related AEs seen in <20% of patients receiving avelumab, all grade 1 or 2 [282-287,293,295-297]

cards.name: Grade 3 or 4 TRAEs (ipilimumab+nivolumab)
cards.detail: Grade 3 or 4 TRAEs observed in 40% (cohort A) and 32% (cohort B) in the randomized phase 2 trial [291]

cards.name: TRAEs (combination therapy)
cards.detail: TRAE incidence higher for combination ipilimumab + nivolumab than nivolumab monotherapy (47% vs 28%) [292]

cards.name: Wound healing complications
cards.detail: Potential for wound healing complications, especially when close to critical structures (eye, mouth)

cards.name: Lymphedema
cards.detail: SLNB itself carries risks including lymphedema (particularly with groin nodal basin)

cards.name: Wound infection
cards.detail: SLNB itself carries risks including wound infection

cards.name: Seroma
cards.detail: SLNB itself carries risks including seroma formation

cards.name: Locoregional recurrence (HILP)
cards.detail: In-transit disease: Hyperthermic isolated limb infusion/perfusion (HILP) carries procedure-specific risks; rates of locoregional recurrence and distant metastasis

cards.name: Distant metastasis (HILP)
cards.detail: In-transit disease: Hyperthermic isolated limb infusion/perfusion (HILP) carries procedure-specific risks; rates of locoregional recurrence and distant metastasis

cards.name: Lymphedema (radiation)
cards.detail: Lymphedema risk, particularly for patients with existing lymphedema or at high risk (high BMI, groin nodal basin, current smoker) [MCC-B]

cards.name: Radiation dermatitis
cards.detail: Skin toxicity (radiation dermatitis) is expected during and after treatment
